definition: ML performance metric measuring proportion of predictions that were correct
Formula associated with a neuron that determines output from inputs
Output of an activation function of a neuron
AI-based system that adjusts behavior in response to changes in operational environment
Deliberate use of adversarial examples to cause an ML model to fail
Software licensing and delivery model where AI is centrally hosted, similar to SaaS for AI
A component within an AI-based system that contains AI functionality
Mathematical representation learned from data that makes predictions or decisions
System that includes AI functionality and may include non-AI components
Systematic errors in an algorithm that systematically affect certain groups or inputs differently than others
Process of labeling data with correct outputs or classifications for training purposes
Capability of an engineered system to acquire, process, create and apply knowledge and skills (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11)
ML function that discovers relationships and patterns between variables in data
Technique for artificially increasing the size of a training dataset by creating variations of existing data
Probabilistic model using Bayes' theorem to compute conditional probabilities
Systematic difference in treatment of certain objects, people or groups compared to others (After ISO/IEC TR 24027)
Very large datasets that are complex in volume, variety and velocity, requiring specialized processing techniques
Statistical resampling technique that draws samples with replacement from a dataset to estimate properties
Conversational AI system designed to simulate human conversation and respond to user queries
ML function that predicts the output class for a given input
ML algorithm or model used to classify inputs into predefined categories or classes
ML function that groups similar data points together without predefined labels
Change in perceived functional performance of an ML model over time caused by changes in user expectations, behavior and operational environment
Technique for summarizing ML functional performance of a classification algorithm
Deep neural network architecture designed to process grid-structured data like images using convolutional layers
Attack on generative AI systems where malicious prompts are injected to manipulate model behavior across contexts
Process of collecting raw data from various sources for use in ML workflows
Systematic errors in data collection, representation or labeling that create imbalances affecting model performance
Change in distribution of input data over time, which can negatively impact functional performance of an operational ML model
Implementation of data preparation activities to provide input data to support training or prediction
Single instance or observation in a dataset containing values for one or more features
Activities to transform raw data into suitable format for ML model training
Technical activities cleaning and transforming raw data including handling missing values, normalization and scaling
Collection of data points with their associated features and labels used for training or evaluation
ML algorithm representing decisions as a tree structure with branches representing feature conditions
ML using deep neural networks to automatically learn complex features from large datasets
Neural network comprised of several layers of neurons (synonym: multi-layer perceptron)
Synthetic media created using deep learning to realistically impersonate or alter a person's appearance or voice
Application of ML to predict software defects or failures based on historical code metrics and quality data
Property of a system where given the same inputs and state, the system always produces the same outputs
Technique for detecting bias by comparing decisions on original scenarios with counterfactual versions where sensitive attributes are swapped
AI models deployed and executed on edge devices rather than centralized servers
Distributed computing approach moving computation and data closer to users and data sources at network edges
Complete pass through entire training dataset during neural network training
AI system using encoded knowledge from domain experts to solve specialized problems
AI systems designed to provide interpretable explanations for their decisions and predictions to users
Interactive, visual, hypothesis-driven process to summarize, explore, and understand main characteristics of data
ML performance metric providing balance between recall and precision
ML model prediction mistakenly predicting the negative class
ML model prediction mistakenly predicting the positive class
Individual measurable attribute of input data used for training
Activity identifying attributes in raw data that best represent underlying relationships for the ML model (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11)
Testing approach focused on verifying AI-based system features and their behavior
Large-scale ML model trained on large datasets using self-supervised learning, designed as versatile base for fine-tuning
AI systems at the forefront of capability and performance, often with significant uncertainty about behavior and impacts
Logic system allowing truth values between completely true and completely false, useful for handling uncertainty
Hypothetical AI system with human-level intelligence capable of understanding and performing any intellectual task (synonym: strong AI)
Type of AI that creates new content by learning patterns from existing data
Specialized processor designed for parallel computation used to accelerate ML model training
Information provided by direct observation and measurement known to be real or true
Parameter used to control training of an ML model or set its configuration
Process of selecting optimal hyperparameter values to improve ML model performance
Autonomous entity that observes environment and acts based on decisions to achieve objectives
Degree of consensus among annotations made by different annotators on the same data (ISO/IEC TS 12791)
Text-to-text generative AI system trained on very large collections of language data
ML algorithm that models linear relationship between input features and continuous output
Deterministic AI-based system with fixed unchanging model that does not change behavior once deployed
Computational procedure that learns patterns from data to make predictions or decisions
Software tools and libraries providing functionality to develop, train and deploy ML models
Functionality of ML-based system including classification, regression, clustering and other predictive tasks
ML function that predicts continuous numerical output values from input features
Abbreviation for Machine Learning System or ML-based System
Set of activities used to develop, deploy and operate an ML model
Testing technique examining confidence scores or probability estimates produced by ML models
Analysis of loss function values during training to understand model convergence and performance
ML model that processes and combines multiple types of input data such as text, images and audio
Practice of having multiple annotators label same data to improve quality and assess agreement
AI focused on a single well-defined task (synonym: weak AI) (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11)
AI field focused on enabling computers to understand, process and generate human language
Network of primitive processing elements connected by weighted links, each producing a value by applying a nonlinear function (synonym: artificial neural network) (ISO/IEC 2382)
Specialized hardware designed to mimic neural architecture and computation patterns of biological brains
Basic processing unit in neural network that receives inputs, applies activation function and produces output
Random variations or errors in data that can negatively impact ML model training and performance
Property of a system where outcome is not uniquely determined by initial conditions
Data point that differs significantly from typical values in dataset, potentially indicating errors or unusual patterns
Generation of ML model that corresponds too closely to training dataset, resulting in poor generalization (After ISO/IEC TR 29119-11)
Neural network with just one layer and one neuron
ML performance metric measuring proportion of predicted positives that were correct (After ISO/IEC TR 29119-11)
ML model already trained on large general-purpose dataset, reusable for specific tasks via fine-tuning
Property of a system based on probabilities where outcomes vary according to probability distributions
Ensemble ML algorithm combining multiple decision trees for improved prediction accuracy
ML performance metric measuring proportion of actual positives that were predicted correctly (synonym: sensitivity) (After ISO/IEC TR 29119-11)
Neural network architecture with connections that loop back on themselves, suitable for sequential data processing
Approach where ML model learns using trial-and-reward feedback loop with environment
ML technique where GenAI dynamically retrieves relevant external information to supplement internal knowledge
Mathematical function defining desired behavior objectives in reinforcement learning
Problem where reinforcement learning agent exploits reward function in unintended ways to maximize short-term rewards
Algorithm for exploring solution space to find optimal or satisfactory solutions to problems
AI system capable of improving its performance through experience without explicit reprogramming
Characteristics defining legally or ethically protected groups that must be controlled to prevent discrimination
NLP task for determining emotional tone or opinion expressed in text data
Sampling technique dividing population into subgroups and sampling proportionally from each to maintain distribution
Hypothetical AI with capabilities exceeding human intelligence across all domains (synonym: artificial superintelligence)
Approach to training ML model using labeled dataset
ML algorithm finding optimal hyperplane to separate classes in classification problems
Hypothetical future point when artificial superintelligence surpasses human intelligence with unpredictable consequences
Parameter controlling randomness and diversity in generative AI model output
Challenge of determining whether a test has passed or failed for given test inputs and state
Dataset used to train an ML model
Neural network architecture processing sequential data to capture long-range dependencies
Prediction where model correctly predicts negative class
Prediction where model correctly predicts positive class
Generation of ML model that does not reflect underlying trend of training dataset, resulting in inaccurate predictions (after ISO/IEC TR 29119-11)
Approach to training ML model using unlabeled dataset
Dataset used to evaluate a trained ML model for the purpose of tuning
Classical computing architecture with separate memory and processing units connected by bus
Internal variable of a connection between neurons affecting computation, changes during training
